prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "demand distribution",
    "purchase price",
    "selling price",
    "salvage value",
    "budget"
  ],
  "domain": "OM",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "The demand distribution is calibrated",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Single order before demand",
    "Lost sales have no later recovery"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "future_or_hidden_information"
  ],
  "id": "om-07",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "nonnegative integer order quantity",
      "budget ceiling"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "preseason order quantity"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected profit",
    "mechanism": "The same order quantity creates leftover cost in low demand and lost margin in high demand.",
    "model_type": "newsvendor",
    "state_variables": [
      "realized demand",
      "leftovers",
      "lost sales"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "seasonal demand"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "Demand is known at ordering time"
  ],
  "prompt": "A buyer selects one order quantity for a short-life seasonal product before demand is observed. Unsold units are salvaged and unmet demand is lost. Write an accessible business problem that retains the one-shot timing and the overage-versus-underage trade-off.",
  "tags": [
    "demand-uncertainty",
    "newsvendor",
    "inventory"
  ],
  "title": "Seasonal order quantity under demand uncertainty"
}
```
